title: Support for large excel file
author: oliverzy
body:
Hi,

I have a questions that I have a very large excel file that has 80w rows and 10 columns?
Is it possible to use this library to process row by row such as nodejs stream module?

comments.author: guyonroche
comments.body:
I take it you mean 80 million rows?
That would be difficult with this library as it is since it is designed as a document editor, not a streaming parser. I did think it would be useful to add streaming writing/parsing as extensions once I've run out of other things to do

comments.author: oliverzy
comments.body: yes，thanks for consideration.

comments.author: guyonroche
comments.body: Just in case you're still watching this issue - I've just published 0.2.0 which is step 1 on the way to your 80M rows. It's not quite there yet as there are limitations imposed by the zip library I'm using that restrict the worksheet to about 20M-30M cells (or 2M-3M rows). Step 2 is to look into a more stream oriented zip library that will compress as I write. I'll look into that next as it should be a fairly easy fix

comments.author: guyonroche
comments.body: Further update: The zipping is fixed and now very large spreadsheets can be written - I tested it with 100M rows of 10 cells each! Still working on the parsing though - running into memory issues.

comments.author: bchr02
comments.body: @guyonroche just wanted to let you know that I have switched to your [repo](https://github.com/guyonroche/exceljs) from [js-xlsx](https://github.com/SheetJS/js-xlsx) simply because of the streaming support. I have testing it out and it works great. Good job! Great Repo!

comments.author: oliverzy
comments.body: wow, sounds great. Thanks for the excellent work!

comments.author: MrHubble
comments.body:
I'm currently using js-xlsx to convert excel files to json but am running into memory issues with larger files (row count wise). Before converting I use js-xlsx to ensure there is only one sheet (a limitation I am enforcing in my app), my headers match the defined template and that the total row count is less than 2,000. 

Would I be able to perform the same process with exceljs streaming (perform some validations on an uploaded file and then convert to json)? I would like to use exceljs streaming to hopefully avoid memory issues.

comments.author: guyonroche
comments.body: I have to admit - I haven't been able to look at this for a while but it is an itch that won't go away. I'm hoping to address this in the next months.

comments.author: alangunning
comments.body: Can't wait :+1:

comments.author: lrecknagel
comments.body:
Is there any option I have to set to enable such large exports.
I try to export 10 columns with 1M rows but getting a JS out of heap issue each time, also when I set:
```bash
--max_old_space_size=4000  
# OR
--max_old_space_size=11000
```

I´ve created a small stress test where I tested some different column-row constellations.
https://gist.github.com/lrecknagel/105cb3399eb599fa90f326441ff615ae

Maybe anyone can give me an advice :)

comments.author: ramesh-joshi
comments.body: Hi @guyonroche, any update on the large file issue?

comments.author: bakhodir10
comments.body: Hi. I have the same issue. It is not generating a file for the rows more than 24k( I am trying to create 100k rows). Any update?

comments.author: pippotantan
comments.body: Hello, may I ask the maximum number of data/rows supported? Thanks.

comments.author: gagantous
comments.body:
hi i tried to solve this with this here some reccomendation

@pippotantan https://stackoverflow.com/a/71215812/7840345

comments.author: tunniclm
comments.body:
@guyonroche I had a need to stream from `knex` to xlsx file to express response stream and was looking at various different libraries. I initially chose to investigate `exceljs` and found quickly that there was some buffering problem when using streaming that seems related to backpressure. I see there are a few other issues mentioning this as well:
- https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/734
- https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/1894

I was able to make some progress on implementing backpressure support here:
- https://github.com/tunniclm/exceljs/tree/row_commit_backpressure_stream

This was enough to solve my use case, but I fear the API changes might make it unsuitable as PR candidate. I'd be happy to try and rework it a little if you think it would be a useful inclusion into `exceljs`.

comments.author: zlooun
comments.body:
So much time has passed, and no one wants to solve such a banal and at the same time important problem. Why waste time on steam if it doesn't work properly anyway?

Solve this problem pls

comments.author: zlooun
comments.body: i solve this problem in this fork: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zlooun/exceljs

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body: @zlooun would you like to open PR and merge your sollution?

comments.body: @zlooun nice solution, thanks
